trade.summary: Giappone is Cina's third-largest trading partner and Cina is Giappone's largest. Both are deeply economically intertwined despite political tensions. Giappone exports vehicles, machinery, electronics, and chemicals. Cina exports electronics, clothing, machinery, and chemicals. Giappone is pursuing supply chain diversification but deep interdependence persists.
disputes: Giappone's semiconductor export controls aligned with Stati Uniti
Fukushima water release controversy (Cina banned Giapponeese seafood)
Rare earth export restrictions by Cina (2010, recurring)

diplomatic.summary: Relations are characterized by deep structural tensions despite economic interdependence. The Senkaku/Diaoyu Islands dispute remains unresolved. Giappone has aligned closely with Stati Uniti on Cina policy, joining export controls on semiconductors and increasing defense spending. Cina has imposed seafood bans in retaliation for Fukushima water release. Historical memories of WWII periodically inflame public opinion.
recent_incidents: Cina imposed ban on Giapponeese seafood imports (Fukushima wastewater, 2023)
Giappone joined Stati Uniti-led semiconductor export controls on Cina
Chinese military aircraft violations of Giapponeese ADIZ (recurring)
Giappone's new National Security Strategy designates Cina as top security challenge (2022)
Chinese coast guard confrontations near Senkaku Islands (recurring)

military.summary: Significant military tension. Cina's frequent military exercises near Taiwan, ADIZ intrusions, and assertive posture in East Cina Sea are primary drivers of Giappone's historic defense buildup. Giappone has increased defense spending to 2% GDP and acquired counterstrike capabilities. Giappone-Stati Uniti alliance provides extended deterrence. No arms transfers. Near-miss incidents recurring.
incidents: Multiple Chinese military aircraft in Japanese ADIZ (2023-2025)
Japanese coast guard confrontation with Chinese survey vessels
Chinese warship transiting through Japan's Osumi Strait

financial.summary: Deep financial interdependence but declining. Giapponeese companies (Toyota, Sony, Honda) are large investors in Cina. Cina holds significant Giapponeese yen-denominated assets. Giappone is diversifying away from Cina, incentivizing factory relocations. Chinese investment in Giappone is limited due to Giappone's FDI screening.

energy.summary: Giappone imports Chinese solar panels and batteries. Cina imports some Giapponeese LNG and processed energy products. Giappone sources some Chinese coal and minerals. Giappone is reducing rare earth dependency on Cina following past export restrictions.

scientific.summary: Historically significant scientific cooperation now under severe scrutiny. Giappone has implemented technology transfer controls aligned with Stati Uniti export control frameworks. Joint publications are declining as Giapponeese universities review Chinese partnerships for dual-use concerns.
